mnemonics: FIRST and FOLLOW Friends Forever - Use FIRST to start, FOLLOW to finish when predicting productions.
Predict, Don't Backtrack - Top-down parsers predict the right production; backtracking is inefficient.
LL(1): Look Left 1 - Only need to look 1 token ahead to make parsing decisions.
keyPoints: Top-down parsing begins with the start symbol and applies productions to derive the input string.
Recursive Descent Parsing implements each non-terminal as a recursive procedure.
LL(1) parsers use a parsing table built from FIRST and FOLLOW sets to make deterministic decisions.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle epsilon (ε) productions when calculating FIRST and FOLLOW sets.
Assuming any grammar can be parsed with a recursive descent parser, when left-recursion must be eliminated first.
Creating ambiguous parsing table entries (multiple productions for the same non-terminal and terminal), which makes a grammar non-LL(1).
